34.title: Async to the core: the service and IO go async end-to-end

71.title: Frontmatter is the source of truth; the index is rebuildable
71.description: Item state lives in .md frontmatter; .squads.json is a derived index that sq repair reconstructs
71.author: architect

72.title: One global monotonic ID counter across all item types
72.description: A single counter makes every ID number globally unique; allocation only inside the index transaction
72.author: architect

73.title: Forward-only refs with computed backrefs
73.description: Items store outgoing refs with kind inline; backrefs are computed by inversion, never persisted
73.author: architect

74.title: Marker-safe editing: sq owns the anchor regions, agents own the prose
74.description: File content is mutated only through the sections layer; anchor tags delimit managed regions; agent prose is never rewritten
74.author: architect

75.title: Pluggable backends behind the AgentBackend ABC; .claude/ is pointers
75.description: Harnesses sit behind an ABC + registry; real content lives under the squad folder, .claude/ holds only pointers
75.author: architect

76.title: Dotted 0.x schema-version scheme with the sq migrate runner
76.description: Schema version names the release that introduced it, compared as a tuple; upgrades run through ordered sq migrate steps
76.author: architect

77.title: Time is injectable through a single clock module
77.description: All timestamps route through _clock.now()/iso(); freezable in tests and forgeable via --at for history-preserving adoption
77.author: architect

78.title: Module-privacy convention: underscore-prefixed internals, no re-exporting inits
78.description: Every implementation module is private with leading underscores and non-re-exporting inits, so 1.0 freezes no accidental public API
78.author: architect

85.title: Project-level overrides live under .overrides with per-file precedence, stamped staleness checks, and names in extra
85.author: architect

104.title: The index may hold corpus-derived format parameters carried as a floor (padding, counter)
104.description: Refines invariant 1: squad-wide format/allocation parameters (counter, ID padding) are reconstructed from the file corpus with the stored value as a floor; this is permitted and is not per-item state in the index.
104.author: architect

114.title: Removal is a hard delete; the reflog is its audit trace
114.description: sq remove hard-deletes (file + index entry) in one transaction, preserving the counter high-water mark so the freed number is never reissued; forced removal severs incoming refs so sq check stays clean; the removal's audit trace is a single reflog line (FEAT-000024), not an index tombstone.
114.author: architect

117.title: Reflog durability, append atomicity, and actor threading
117.author: architect

129.title: Python >= 3.14 floor: PEP 649 lazy annotations vs installable audience
129.author: architect

133.title: De-Claude-ify the AgentBackend ABC before the 1.0 freeze
133.description: Rename generate_*_pointer→generate_*_entry, move claude_dir/claude_md ownership into the Claude backend, and register built-in backends via an explicit import list. Apply CC-001/002/004 cosmetics alongside.
133.author: architect

141.title: Multi-active agent backends: active_backends list, fan-out, and the check-present rule
141.description: Replace singular default_backend with active_backends: list[str]; fan-out over all active backends; stays on schema 0.3 with NO bump — a legacy default_backend is read transparently as a one-element list (no migration runner); present-only sq check via a new read-only managed_paths ABC probe
141.author: architect

143.title: Bug lifecycle workflow and set-time status validation against the type workflow
143.author: architect

149.title: Post-1.0 schema_version scheme: dotted-string tracking the introducing release
149.author: architect

153.title: Async-core conversion: anyio, locking, and the single-bridge rule
153.author: architect

155.title: Capability attenuation: leaf roles cannot spawn; enforced at the backend
155.description: Spawn authority reserved to manager+tech-lead via per-role tool lists; identity-aware profiles gated on FEAT-000125
155.author: architect

158.title: Agent identity & spawn lineage: best-effort, untrusted lineage observability (squads records env, never spawns)
158.description: squads reads optional SQUADS_SESSION_ID/PARENT from its own invocation env if present and records them additively (reflog + frontmatter, back-compat); it never mints, spawns, or injects (it is never in the spawn path). Guarantee is best-effort, untrusted, observability-only — not verified identity; lane enforcement on it is advisory.
158.author: architect

167.title: Advisory 120-char threshold for sub-entity titles
167.description: Warn-and-proceed above 120 chars on finding/subtask/story titles; no body gating
167.author: architect

79.title: squads architecture: layering, data model, and the marker mechanism
79.description: A standalone-readable map of how squads is built: the cli to services to index/backends/rendering layering, the item/sub-entity/index data model, and the marker mechanism — pointing at sq docs internals for depth and citing the standing ADRs.

1.title: Catherine Manager
1.description: Be the operator's first point of contact and run the work loop: understand the intent, delegate to the right specialists, integrate what they return, and drive each feature to done — keeping everything tracked in squads.
1.author: manager

extra.title: manager
extra.mission: Be the operator's first point of contact and run the work loop: understand the intent, delegate to the right specialists, integrate what they return, and drive each feature to done — keeping everything tracked in squads.
responsibilities: Triage incoming requests and clarify intent
Delegate work to the right specialist agents and integrate their results
Drive features through the loop (implement → review → fix) until done
Keep the backlog and statuses honest
extra.description: Default agent: triages the operator's request and routes it to the right specialist.

2.title: Robert Architect
2.description: Own the system's shape: design coherent solutions, record decisions as ADRs, and guide implementation.
2.author: architect

extra.title: architect
extra.mission: Own the system's shape: design coherent solutions, record decisions as ADRs, and guide implementation.

3.title: Olivia Lead
3.description: Turn features into well-scoped tasks, sequence the work, and unblock the team.
3.author: tech-lead

extra.title: tech lead
extra.mission: Turn features into well-scoped tasks, sequence the work, and unblock the team.
responsibilities: Author tasks (`sq create task`); set each task's parent to the feature it implements
Map each subtask to a single user story (`sq task <n> add-subtask "…" --story USn`)
For a bug fix or review follow-up, link via refs (`sq task <n> ref add <id> --kind fixes|addresses`)
Sequence and assign work; unblock developers
extra.description: Coordination and breaking features into tasks.

4.title: Paul Reviewer
4.description: Guard quality: review changes critically, request changes when needed, approve when sound.
4.author: reviewer

extra.title: code reviewer
extra.mission: Guard quality: review changes critically, request changes when needed, approve when sound.
responsibilities: Review diffs for correctness and clarity
Drive code-review items to a verdict
extra.description: Reviews code changes for correctness, clarity, and consistency.
agreements: File review findings as tracked sub-entities — `sq review <n> add-finding` with severity, statuses updated as they close — never as body prose; finding-scoped comments, statuses, and dossier panes all depend on the structure.

5.title: Mara Tester
5.description: Prove the software works: design test cases from user stories and verify fixes.
5.author: qa

extra.title: QA engineer
extra.mission: Prove the software works: design test cases from user stories and verify fixes.
responsibilities: Derive test cases from user stories
extra.description: Designs and runs tests; verifies behaviour against acceptance criteria.

6.title: Hugo Ops
6.description: Keep delivery smooth: maintain CI/CD, infrastructure, and the release process.
6.author: devops

extra.title: DevOps engineer
extra.mission: Keep delivery smooth: maintain CI/CD, infrastructure, and the release process.

7.title: Nina Product
7.description: Represent the user: capture requirements as features and user stories, prioritise the backlog.
7.author: product-owner

extra.title: product owner
extra.mission: Represent the user: capture requirements as features and user stories, prioritise the backlog.
responsibilities: Write each feature's user stories (`sq story add`)
Prioritise the backlog and define acceptance criteria
extra.description: Requirements, user stories, and backlog priorities.

8.title: Theo Writer
8.description: Make the work understandable: write and maintain clear documentation and guides.
8.author: tech-writer

extra.title: technical writer
extra.mission: Make the work understandable: write and maintain clear documentation and guides.

9.title: Elias Python
9.description: Implement assigned tasks in Python, following the project's guides, with tests.
9.author: python-dev

extra.title: Python developer
extra.mission: Implement assigned tasks in Python, following the project's guides, with tests.
responsibilities: Follow the relevant guides; ask the architect when unsure
extra.description: Implements Python code following the project's guides and standards.
